# Prompt Log - 12-20-2025_07-09

## Task: Create Simple User Interface for Text Adventure Games

### Objective
Create a simple user interface in the Dungine WPF application to play Zork-like text adventure games.

### Changes Made

#### 1. MainWindow.xaml - Created Text Adventure UI
- **Dark Theme Design**: Applied modern dark theme (#1E1E1E background) for better visual experience
- **Title Bar**: Added "UBIQUITUS TEXT ADVENTURE" header with distinctive teal color (#00D4AA)
- **Game Output Area**: 
  - Created scrollable text output area with dark background (#252526)
  - Implemented using TextBlock with Consolas font for classic terminal feel
  - Auto-scrolls to show latest output
  - Proper text wrapping for long descriptions
- **Command Input**: 
  - Prompt indicator (">") in teal to match theme
  - Text input box styled with dark theme
  - Send button with hover effects and rounded corners
  - Enter key support for command submission

#### 2. MainWindow.xaml.cs - Game Engine Integration
- **Game Engine Integration**: Connected TextGame.GameEngine to the UI
- **Event Handling**: Subscribed to OutputGenerated event for displaying game output
- **Sample World Creation**: Implemented CreateSampleWorld() method with:
  - 4 connected rooms (Entrance Hall, Library, Garden, Cellar)
  - 3 interactive items (key, book, statue)
  - Proper room connections (north, south, east, west, up, down)
- **Command Processing**:
  - Processes user input through game engine
  - Displays commands with "> " prefix
  - Clears input and maintains focus after each command
  - Full support for all TextGame engine commands (go, look, take, examine, use, inventory, help, quit)

#### 3. Ubiquitus.csproj - Project Configuration
- **Project Reference**: Added reference to GameLibrary\TextGame\TextGame.csproj
- **Build Configuration**: 
  - Disabled auto-generation of assembly info attributes to prevent build conflicts
  - Disabled TargetFramework attribute generation
  - Maintained existing version information and documentation settings

### Sample Game Features
The implemented sample world includes:
- **Entrance Hall**: Starting location with a brass key
- **Library**: Contains an ancient book
- **Garden**: Features a marble statue (not takeable)
- **Cellar**: Dark location accessible from entrance

### Supported Commands
The UI supports all standard text adventure commands:
- Movement: go [direction], north, south, east, west, up, down
- Actions: look, examine [item], take [item], drop [item], use [item]
- Info: inventory/i, help/?
- Game: quit/exit

### Technical Details
- **UI Framework**: WPF with XAML
- **Game Engine**: TextGame library with ID-based architecture
- **Design Pattern**: Event-driven architecture for game output
- **Threading**: Uses Dispatcher.Invoke for thread-safe UI updates

### Build Status
? Build successful
? All errors resolved
? Game engine properly integrated
? UI fully functional

- Cartridge system for loading external game files
